app.title: NuxtEdge
app.description: A production-ready Nuxt template with SQLite database, blob storage, KV store, and caching, all running at the edge.

seo.title: NuxtEdge - Build on the Edge
seo.description: A production-ready Nuxt app with database, blob storage, KV store, and caching. Deploy globally in seconds.

hero.description: A production-ready Nuxt app with database, blob storage, KV store, and caching. Deploy globally in seconds.

section.headline: Primitives
section.title: Everything you need, nothing you don't

db.title: Database
db.description: Full SQLite at the edge with Drizzle ORM. Zero cold starts, full CRUD, automatic migrations.

kv.title: KV Store
kv.description: Lightning-fast key-value storage globally replicated, perfect for config, flags, and redirects.

cache.title: Cache
cache.description: Built-in Nitro caching with TTL, stale-while-revalidate, and per-route invalidation. Zero config.

i18n.title: Internationalization
i18n.description: Configure locales in Nuxt and store translation strings in locale JSON files for each language.

email.title: Email
email.description: Send transactional email from the edge with a provider-ready foundation for notifications and workflows.

agents.title: AI DX
agents.description: Preinstalled Agent Skills make it easier to build pages, storage features, and repo improvements in this app.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.description: Session, login, and route-protection primitives are planned next.

seo.title: SEO
seo.description: Metadata, social tags, and search-friendly defaults are planned next.

cta.title: Deploy to the edge in seconds
cta.description: NuxtEdge connects your Nuxt app to Cloudflare's global network. One command to deploy database migrations, blob storage, and your full application.

seo.title: AI DX · NuxtEdge
seo.description: How to use the preinstalled Agent Skills in this repo, with practical prompts and examples for faster development.

hero.title: Use AI Agents
hero.lead: This repo already ships with useful Agent Skills and coding techniques. The main idea is simple: ask for product work in plain language, and use slash-style skill hints like `/nuxt`, `/nuxt-ui`, or `/nuxthub` when you want to steer the implementation.

repo.title: What is available in this repo
repo.subtitle: Three repo-level skills cover most of the work here.

installedSkills.workflowHelper: The default repo workflow helper. Good for general tasks when you want AI to pick the right path.
installedSkills.nuxt: Best for pages, layouts, server routes, composables, and Nuxt-specific implementation details.
installedSkills.nuxthub: Use this for DB, KV, blob, cache, and other edge-backed full-stack features.

promptExamples.title: Prompt examples
promptExamples.subtitle: These are closer to what you would actually type.

buildPage.title: Build a page
buildPage.prompt: /nuxt create a new `/contact` page and wire it into the navbar.

polishUi.title: Polish a UI
polishUi.prompt: /nuxt-ui improve this settings screen and make the layout feel more intentional.

addStorage.title: Add storage
addStorage.prompt: /nuxthub add a favorites feature stored in the database with a matching API route and UI.

improveDemo.title: Improve a demo
improveDemo.prompt: /nuxt /nuxthub improve the blob demo UI and keep upload/delete fully working.

goodPrompts.title: How to get better results
goodPrompts.subtitle: A few small prompt habits help a lot.
goodPrompts.knowArea: Use `/nuxt`, `/nuxt-ui`, or `/nuxthub` when you already know the area of work.
goodPrompts.mentionRoute: Mention the route, file, or feature you want changed.
goodPrompts.describeOutcome: Describe the outcome you want, not just the tool.
goodPrompts.verifyChange: Ask Codex to verify when the change should be typechecked or tested.
goodPrompts.goodExample: Good: `/nuxthub` add a simple favorites API and wire it into the UI.

seo.title: Internationalization · NuxtEdge
seo.description: A short guide to configuring languages in Nuxt and organizing locale files in this repo.

lead.after: then keep the translation strings in

configureLocales.title: 1. Configure locales
configureLocales.body: Add a new language by adding one more locale entry here. This is where Nuxt decides the locale code, label, and JSON file name.

addTranslationFile.title: 2. Add the translation file
addTranslationFile.body: Each file is just a JSON dictionary. Keep the same keys across locales and translate only the values.

module.title: Module
module.body: Make sure `{'@'}nuxtjs/i18n` is enabled in the Nuxt modules list.

config.title: Config
config.body: Register each locale in `nuxt.config.ts` with its code and file.

seo.title: Email · NuxtEdge
seo.description: How to configure `nuxt-nodemailer`, set `sendMail` params, and override secrets with environment variables.

hero.title: Email
hero.lead: Send transactional email from the server runtime using the preinstalled `nuxt-nodemailer` module. This page documents the config keys, the `sendMail` params, and the env overrides you need before wiring a real notification flow.

configureTransporter.title: 1. Configure the transporter
configureTransporter.body: The template already includes a `nodemailer` block in `nuxt.config.ts`. Replace the example values with your provider credentials. The module only lets env vars override keys that are already present in config, so keep the full shape in place.

sendMailParams.title: 2. Set the `sendMail` params
sendMailParams.body: Use `useNodeMailer()` inside a server route, server action, or another server-only context. `sendMail()` automatically inherits `from` from your Nuxt config, so most calls only need the recipient and content.

envOverrides.title: 3. Override secrets with env vars
envOverrides.body: Prefer env vars for secrets and production-specific values. Prefix each override with `NUXT_NODEMAILER_` and uppercase the path. Nested keys flatten with underscores.

recommendation.title: 4. Provider recommendation

body.after: for transactional email. It has a cleaner developer workflow than many legacy SMTP providers, good deliverability, and straightforward API key management. If you prefer pure SMTP portability, Postmark, Mailgun, or Mailtrap also fit this template well.

baseline.label: Recommended baseline:
baseline.secretPrefix: and put the real secret in

recommendation.footer: Docs-first for now. A future upgrade can add a setup validator or a test-send endpoint.

configFields.host: Your SMTP host from the provider.
configFields.port: Usually 587 for STARTTLS or 465 for implicit TLS.
configFields.authUser: SMTP username or API key, depending on provider.
configFields.authPass: Secret credential. Prefer env overrides in deployment.

sendMailParams.subject: Required subject line.
sendMailParams.text: Recommended for clients that do not render HTML.
sendMailParams.from: Usually omit this because the module inherits it from config.

seo.title: Blob Storage · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Upload and serve files with automatic CDN caching and global delivery through NuxtHub Blob storage.

lead.beforeCode: Upload and serve files with automatic CDN caching. Powered by
lead.afterCode: stores to Cloudflare R2 with global edge delivery.

upload.label: Drop an image here
upload.description: PNG, JPG, or WebP up to 8MB. Click to browse or drag and drop.

selectedFileHint.beforeCode: Selected file is uploaded to R2 through

empty.title: No images yet
empty.description: Upload your first image above to store it in Cloudflare R2.

infoCards.title: Edge CDN
infoCards.text: Files served from the nearest Cloudflare PoP - 300+ locations worldwide.

infoCards.title: Secure by default
infoCards.text: CSP headers automatically applied. No direct R2 bucket access required.

infoCards.title: No size limits
infoCards.text: Store anything from tiny config files to large video assets at the edge.

seo.title: Cache · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Explore built-in Nitro response caching with TTL, cache hits, and request history.

lead.beforeBadge: Built-in Nitro response caching with configurable TTL. This endpoint is cached for
lead.afterBadge: - watch the value stay identical across requests, then reset after TTL expires.

demo.title: Live Cache Demo
demo.idle: Hit the button to make your first request

note.afterCode: stays the same within 5s - that's the cache. After TTL, a new random value is computed.

concepts.title: cachedEventHandler
concepts.desc: Wraps any Nitro handler with automatic response caching. Supports TTL, cache keys, and groups.

concepts.title: Stale-while-revalidate
concepts.desc: Serve stale data instantly while refreshing in the background. Zero latency for users.

concepts.title: Cache invalidation
concepts.desc: Granular control via cache groups. Bust specific routes or entire feature groups on demand.

seo.title: Database · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Manage SQLite data at the edge with a simple CRUD demo and schema view.

db.title: Database
db.lead: Full CRUD on a SQLite database at the edge, with support for any SQL database.

migrations.title: Migration note

d1.beforeCode: For Cloudflare D1, migrations cannot run during build because there is no database connection in CI. Run
d1.afterCode: locally or add a CI step to apply migrations before deployment.

migrations.external: For external databases such as Postgres or MySQL, migrations are applied automatically when a database connection is available during development and build.
migrations.autoIntro: Migrations are automatically applied when you:

composer.placeholder: Write a message to store in SQLite...

empty.title: No messages yet
empty.description: Send your first message above to store it in SQLite.

seo.title: KV Store · NuxtEdge
seo.description: Store redirect rules in NuxtHub KV and apply them at the edge through Nitro middleware.

lead.beforeCode: KV is useful for small, fast reads like redirects, feature flags, app config, and lightweight edge state. This demo stores redirect rules in
lead.afterCode: so Nitro can apply them on request.

editor.title: Redirect Rules
editor.badge: Managed via KV · Applied by Nitro
editor.empty: No redirect rules. Add one below.
editor.footerNote: Changes apply on next request via Nitro middleware.

steps.title: Store in KV
steps.desc: Redirect rules are saved as a JSON object in NuxtEdge KV with hubKV().set().

steps.title: Nitro middleware
steps.desc: A server middleware reads the KV store on every request and matches paths.

steps.title: Edge redirect
steps.desc: Matching requests are redirected with sendRedirect() - no origin hit needed.
